entries.content: Three ratios decompose the relationship between corrected and conventional corporate profit rates. x1 = 1 + (NMINTcorp/Pcorp) captures the imputed interest adjustment. x2 = 1 + (INVcorp(-1)/KNCcorpbea(-1)) captures the inventory adjustment to capital stock. x3 = KNCcorpbea(-1)/KGCcorp(-1) captures the net/gross capital stock revaluation via GPIM. The relationship is: rcorp/rcorpnipa ≈ x3 * (x1/x2).
entries.source_ref: Appendix 6.8, Table II-7, equations in Ch. 6 text

entries.content: Components derive from BEA NIPA Table 7.11 (net monetary interest), Fixed Asset Tables 6.1-6.8 (net vs gross capital stocks), and IRS Statistics of Income (corporate inventories). The x1 ratio rose from ~1.08 in 1948 to ~1.71 in 1982 as financial intermediation grew relative to profits.
entries.source_ref: BEA NIPA T7.11, FA, IRS SOI

entries.content: Fig 6.3 shows the three component ratios 1947-2011. x1 (interest adjustment) shows the most dramatic trend, rising sharply through the early 1980s then partially declining. x2 (inventory ratio) is relatively stable near 1.14. x3 (net/gross stock ratio) trends downward from ~1.11 to ~0.61 as the net/gross distinction grows with capital aging.

citations.full: Bureau of Economic Analysis. NIPA Table 7.11: Monetary Interest Paid and Received by Sector.
Bureau of Economic Analysis. Fixed Assets Tables 6.1-6.8: Current-Cost Net and Gross Stock of Private Fixed Assets.

methodology_summary: S028 extracts three component ratio columns from S013 (Table II-7): S013P (x1, interest adjustment), S013Q (x2, inventory ratio), S013R (x3, net/gross revaluation). These ratios provide the analytical decomposition of why Shaikh's corrected profit rate differs from the conventional NIPA measure. The product x3*(x1/x2) approximately equals the ratio rcorp/rcorpnipa.
known_issues: x1 frozen post-2013 because NMINTcorp data from BEA T7.11 is incomplete for recent years
